docs.text:
Feature specifications with test results from the latest run.

6 features 30 scenarios ✅ 4 passed ❌ 11 failed ⬜ 15 no results Feature Scenarios Passed Failed Status User Login Journey 4 0 0 ⬜ User CRUD API 7 0 0 ⬜ Cross-Service Order Flow 4 0 0 ⬜ RAG Pipeline Quality 5 0 5 ❌ Prompt Safety Guardrails 2 1 1 ❌ Summarization Prompt Quality 8 3 5 ❌

docs.title: User Login
docs.text: Expand all Collapse all As a registered user I want to log in to the application So that I can access my dashboard and protected resources Background: Given a registered user exists with email "testuser@example.com" Scenario: Successful login with valid credentials@smoke Given I am on the login page No test results available When I enter email "testuser@example.com" No test results available And I enter password "ValidPassword123!" No test results available And I click the login button No test results available Then I should be redirected to the dashboard No test results available And I should see a welcome message containing my name No test results available Scenario: Login fails with invalid password Given I am on the login page No test results available When I enter email "testuser@example.com" No test results available And I enter password "WrongPassword" No test results available And I click the login button No test results available Then I should see an error message "Invalid email or password" No test results available And I should remain on the login page No test results available Scenario: Login fails with non-existent email Given I am on the login page No test results available When I enter email "nobody@example.com" No test results available And I enter password "AnyPassword123" No test results available And I click the login button No test results available Then I should see an error message "Invalid email or password" No test results available Scenario: Login validation for empty fields Given I am on the login page No test results available When I enter email "<email>" No test results available And I enter password "<password>" No test results available And I click the login button No test results available Then I should see a validation error for "<field>" emailpasswordfield ValidPassword123email testuser@example.compassword email

docs.title: User Crud
Cross Service Order
docs.text: Expand all Collapse all As a system I want orders to propagate correctly across user, order, and inventory services So that data remains consistent across all microservices Background: Given the user service is available at USER_SERVICE_URL And the order service is available at ORDER_SERVICE_URL And the inventory service is available at INVENTORY_SERVICE_URL Scenario: Successful order decreases inventory@smoke Given a user "Order Buyer" exists in the user service No test results available And product "WIDGET-001" has 50 units in inventory No test results available When I create an order for user with 3 units of "WIDGET-001" No test results available Then the order service should return status 201 No test results available And the order should have status "pending" No test results available And eventually the inventory for "WIDGET-001" should be 47 units No test results available Scenario: Order rejected for non-existent user When I create an order for non-existent user "ghost-user-id" with 1 unit of "WIDGET-001" No test results available Then the order service should return status 400 No test results available And the order error code should be "INVALID_USER" No test results available Scenario: Order rejected when insufficient inventory Given a user "Low Stock Buyer" exists in the user service No test results available And product "RARE-001" has 2 units in inventory No test results available When I create an order for user with 5 units of "RARE-001" No test results available Then the order service should return status 409 No test results available And the order error code should be "INSUFFICIENT_INVENTORY" No test results available Scenario: Order status updates propagate to user service@eventual-consistency Given a user "Status Watcher" exists in the user service No test results available And product "WIDGET-002" has 100 units in inventory No test results available When I create an order for user with 1 units of "WIDGET-002" No test results available And the order is marked as "shipped" No test results available Then eventually the user service should show the order as "shipped" No test results available

docs.title: Rag Quality
docs.text:
Expand all Collapse all As a system owner I want the RAG pipeline to retrieve relevant context and generate grounded answers So that users get accurate, source-backed responses Scenario: RAG answer is faithful to retrieved context@faithfulness Given the question "What are the economic costs of climate inaction?" PASSED And the retrieved context: 1ms PASSED 1ms 
      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: 1ms PASSED 1ms 
      According to the IPCC report, the economic cost of climate inaction is
      estimated at $23 trillion by 2050. In comparison, implementing recommended
      mitigation strategies would cost approximately $4 trillion, making proactive
      action significantly more cost-effective.
Then the faithfulness score should be >= 0.7 5190ms FAILED 5190ms 
Error: OpenAI API key is not set. Set the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable or add `apiKey` to the provider config.
   at tests/prompt-eval/ts-steps/prompt-eval.steps.ts:166

  164 |
  165 | Then('the faithfulness score should be >= {float}', async ({}, _threshold: number) => {
> 166 |   const result = await matchesFactuality(
      |                  ^
  167 |     ragCtx.question,
  168 |     ragCtx.retrievedContexts.join('\n'),
  169 |     ragCtx.ragAnswer,

      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: PASSED 
      According to the IPCC report, the economic cost of climate inaction is
      estimated at $23 trillion by 2050. In comparison, implementing recommended
      mitigation strategies would cost approximately $4 trillion.
Then the answer relevancy score should be >= 0.7 5192ms FAILED 5192ms

      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: PASSED 
      Climate inaction costs $23 trillion by 2050 versus $4 trillion for mitigation.
Then the context precision score should be >= 0.6 5176ms FAILED 5176ms

Scenario: Retrieved context contains needed information@context-recall Given the question "What are the economic costs of climate inaction?" 1ms PASSED 1ms And the retrieved context: PASSED 
      The economic cost of inaction is estimated at $23 trillion by 2050,
      compared to $4 trillion for implementing the recommended mitigation strategies.
And the RAG answer: PASSED 
      Climate inaction costs $23 trillion by 2050 versus $4 trillion for mitigation.
And the reference answer "Climate inaction costs $23 trillion by 2050. Mitigation costs $4 trillion." PASSED Then the context recall score should be >= 0.7 5176ms FAILED 5176ms
